summary: Ukrainian UAV activity intensified, striking deep into Russian territory including Yaroslavl refinery and multiple oblasts, while Russia reported downing 94 drones in five hours. Russia escalated missile use, deploying Iskander‑M systems from Taganrog and conducting guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk. Ground combat remains active with Russian advances near Kupyansk and Ukrainian counter‑attacks around Volokhovka.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck Yaroslavl's Slavneft‑YANOS refinery, a top‑six Russian plant processing 15 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81318).
Russia’s air defenses downed 94 Ukrainian UAVs over several Russian regions within five hours (Source: @tass_agency/376729).
Iskander‑M ballistic missiles were positioned in Taganrog, Rostov region, threatening Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839).
A ballistic missile hit central Pryluky, killing two, injuring 17, and damaging a commercial enterprise and nearby shopping centre (Source: @UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian forces launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @kpszsu/62290).
UAV groups reported moving across Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv oblasts, targeting towns such as Bozhedarivka, Kremenchuk, and Vylnohirs’k (Source: @eRadarrua/92731, @eRadarrua/92723).
Russian reports claim advances of the North Army Group up to 600 m on the Sumy front and liberation of Volokhovka on the Kharkiv front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian Foreign Ministry denied using Latvian territory for drone launches, despite Russian intelligence alleging bases in Adazi and others (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30284).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAVs struck the strategic Yaroslavl refinery and multiple Ukrainian front‑line towns, prompting Russia to report downing 94 drones in five hours. Russia escalated missile deployments, positioning Iskander‑M systems in Taganrog and conducting guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk. Ground combat remains fluid, with Russian forces advancing near Kupyansk while Ukrainian units reclaim villages such as Volokhovka.

points: Ukrainian drone units attacked the Slavneft‑YANOS refinery in Yaroslavl, a plant processing roughly 15 million tonnes of oil annually, marking a deep strike into Russian industrial heartland (Source: @UkraineNow/81318).

A ballistic missile strike on Pryluky’s centre killed two civilians, injured at least 17, and damaged a local enterprise, a shopping centre, and a supermarket, highlighting Ukraine’s vulnerability to long‑range attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian forces employed guided aerial bombs against targets in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, causing infrastructure damage and civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/62290).

UAV activity was reported across multiple front‑line oblasts: groups moved from Poltava toward Bozhedarivka, Kremenchuk, and from Sumy toward Shostka and Królevets, indicating coordinated reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/92731, @eRadarrua/92723).
Ukrainian intelligence denied operating drones from Latvian bases, despite Russian claims of deployments at Adazi, Selija, Lielvarde, Daugavpils, and Jekabpils (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30284).
On the Sumy front, Russian reports state Ukrainian assault units advanced up to 600 m in nineteen sectors, suggesting intense localized fighting (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian forces reportedly recaptured the village of Volokhovka on the Kharkiv direction, pushing back Russian units of the 126th Motor Rifle Regiment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian drone attacks caused civilian injuries in Kherson region, with nine districts losing electricity due to UAV strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian air defence systems intercepted 94 Ukrainian UAVs across several Russian regions within a five‑hour window, demonstrating heightened anti‑drone activity (Source: @tass_agency/376729).
Russia positioned Iskander‑M ballistic missile launchers in Taganrog, Rostov oblast, raising the threat level for Ukrainian deep‑strike capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839).

The Russian Defence Ministry reported extensive UAV downings (651) and repelled attacks on the industrial zone of Nevinnomyssk in Stavropol, indicating sustained aerial pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian forces claimed missile attacks on Belgorod and drone strikes on trucks and civilians in the Belgorod region, reflecting ongoing cross‑border hostilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
In the Rostov region, Russian reports state about two dozen UAVs were destroyed across four districts, showing concentrated air‑defence efforts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian troops continued offensive actions on the East‑Zaporizhia front, aiming to liberate Verkhnya Tersa, Vozdvizhevka, and Komsomolskoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
On the Kupyansk front, Russian forces are breaking Ukrainian defenses along the east bank of the Oskol River, indicating a push in the southern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).

Russian statements at the UN Security Council emphasized that Russia will pursue SVO objectives until Ukraine meets a list of political conditions, underscoring a hardline stance (Source: @UkraineNow/81330).
Ukrainian UAV groups reported operating from Sumy toward Shostka and Królevets, suggesting reconnaissance and strike missions in the eastern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/92731).
Russian reports indicate Ukrainian assault units advanced up to 600 m on the Sumy direction across nineteen sectors, highlighting intense localized combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
On the Kharkiv front, Ukrainian forces reclaimed Volokhovka, pushing back the 126th Motor Rifle Regiment of Russia’s 71st Division (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian guided aerial bomb strikes targeted Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, causing infrastructure damage and civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/62290).
Ukrainian drone attacks on the industrial zone of Nevinnomyssk in Stavropol were repelled, showing cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian forces reported continued offensive actions on the East‑Zaporizhia front, aiming to capture Verkhnya Tersa and surrounding settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted near Kremenchuk and Bozhedarivka in Poltava oblast, indicating pressure on Russian supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/92731).
Russian air defenses downed numerous UAVs over the Leningrad region, reflecting expanded aerial engagements far from the front line (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from Poltava toward Orzhitsia in Sumy oblast, indicating north‑west to north‑east penetration (Source: @eRadarrua/92731).
Russian reports state 651 enemy drones were downed, with significant activity over the Leningrad region, suggesting northern aerial threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV groups from Sumy were observed heading toward Chernihiv, targeting areas near Nizhyn and Nosivky (Source: @kpszsu/62261).
A new UAV group was noted on the northern edge of Kherson moving northward, potentially threatening the northern Kherson corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62307).
Russian air defence claimed 94 UAVs shot down across multiple Russian regions, including northern oblasts, within five hours (Source: @tass_agency/376729).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV incident near Kremenchuk, Poltava, indicating activity in the north‑central sector (Source: @eRadarrua/92731).
Russian reports of drone attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Belgorod district underscore the northern border’s volatility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Chernihiv region heading toward Nizhyn, suggesting reconnaissance of supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62294).
Russian forces are advancing on the east bank of the Oskol River near Kupyansk, breaking Ukrainian defenses in the southern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused civilian injuries in Kherson region, with nine districts losing electricity due to sustained strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian guided aerial bomb strikes hit Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, indicating southern‑central air‑strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/62290).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported near Vylnohirs’k in Dnipropetrovsk oblast, suggesting pressure on southern logistics hubs (Source: @eRadarrua/92719).
Russian reports of drone attacks on a gas station in Bryansk’s Smorovaya Buda village, injuring civilians, show cross‑border southern incursions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian forces faced heavy UAV attacks on the Kamensko‑Dneprovsky municipal district, damaging a central district hospital (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian missile attacks targeted Belgorod, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, reflecting southern front escalation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the southern front’s Oskol River area, indicating reconnaissance ahead of Russian advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russia’s air defence downed 94 Ukrainian UAVs across several regions within five hours, showing intensified anti‑drone operations (Source: @tass_agency/376729).
Ukrainian forces launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk, demonstrating use of precision strike munitions (Source: @kpszsu/62290).
Russian Defence Ministry reported 651 enemy drones downed, with notable engagements over Nevinnomyssk, Leningrad, and Stavropol regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV groups operated across Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv oblasts, targeting logistical nodes and forward positions (Source: @eRadarrua/92731).
Iskander‑M ballistic missile systems were positioned in Taganrog, enhancing Russia’s long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839).
Ukrainian ballistic missile strike on Pryluky caused civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, indicating continued use of long‑range missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81317).
Russian air‑defence claims of downed UAVs over the Leningrad and Belgorod regions illustrate the widening geographic scope of the aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian drone attacks on the oil refinery in Kstovo, Nizhny Novgorod region, were repelled, showing strategic targeting of Russian energy assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones struck Yaroslavl's Slavneft‑YANOS refinery, a top‑six Russian plant processing 15 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81318).
Russia’s air defence downed 94 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple regions within five hours, indicating heightened anti‑drone activity (Source: @tass_agency/376729).
Iskander‑M ballistic missile launchers were deployed in Taganrog, Rostov oblast, raising the long‑range threat to Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66839).
A ballistic missile hit central Pryluky, killing two civilians and injuring 17, damaging a commercial enterprise and nearby shopping centre (Source: @UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian forces used guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @kpszsu/62290).
Russian reports claim Ukrainian assault units advanced up to 600 m on the Sumy front across nineteen sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian troops recaptured Volokhovka on the Kharkiv direction, pushing back Russian 126th Motor Rifle Regiment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have expanded deep into Russian territory, targeting critical energy infrastructure and urban centers, while Russia counters with robust air‑defence and the deployment of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles. Ground combat remains fluid, with Russian forces pressing on the southern Kupyansk axis and Ukrainian units achieving localized gains in the east. The convergence of intensified aerial strikes and missile deployments raises the risk of broader escalation and higher civilian casualties.
sections.regional_response: Western allies continue to provide air‑defence systems and intelligence, yet Russia’s claims of increased UAV interceptions suggest a tightening of its protective envelope. Regional actors, notably NATO, monitor the Iskander‑M deployment as a potential destabilizer for neighboring states. Ukraine’s diplomatic denial of operations from Latvian bases aims to prevent further regional escalation, while Russia leverages the narrative of foreign‑backed drone threats to justify heightened security measures.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV tracking indicates coordinated groups moving from Poltava toward Sumy and Chernihiv, likely supporting reconnaissance for upcoming offensives. The concentration of Russian anti‑drone assets in the north and west suggests anticipation of expanded Ukrainian aerial campaigns. Monitoring of Iskander‑M launch sites in Taganrog is critical, as any activation could shift the strategic balance on the southern front.
